findings.title: safe_log_line's credential-redaction contract is not process-wide: stdlib logging and uncaught exceptions bypass it entirely and land in the agent-readable runtime/ mount
findings.why_it_matters: The security decision this PR documents is: 'every engine log line is produced by safe_log_line, which refuses ... any line matching SECRET_PATTERNS ... runtime/logs/ is agent-readable by design, so a leaked credential in a log line would defeat the credential-isolation decision' (engine/executor.py docstring). That guarantee only holds for code paths that explicitly call self.log(...)/safe_log_line(...). Two mechanisms in this diff write directly to the same agent-readable sink without ever calling safe_log_line: (1) engine/market_feed.py uses Python's stdlib `logging` module (`logger.warning(...)`) with no handler configured anywhere in the repo, so Python's 'handler of last resort' prints those records straight to stderr; and (2) the launchd unit (ops/com.iamnotatrader.engine.plist) redirects the process's raw stdout/stderr to runtime/logs/engine.log and runtime/logs/engine.err.log -- inside the exact runtime/ tree that docs/nanoclaw-setup.md mounts read-write into the NanoClaw agent container. Because engine/main.py has no top-level try/except around main()/run_forever(), ANY uncaught exception -- not just from market_feed.py's warnings -- gets Python's default traceback treatment (exception type + str(exc)) written verbatim to engine.err.log. The two try/except blocks that do exist (EngineLoop.run_once and _on_bucket_rollover) correctly route their error strings through safe_log_line, but they do not cover engine startup (startup()/build_loop()) or, once the documented future wiring in docs/phase2-runbook.md Section 3 step 4 lands, the LiveCredentials.load() / derive_l2() / build_venue_client() call sites that will handle the raw wallet private key. derive_l2's vendor calls are explicitly marked 'GUESS' for exact behavior (unverified against the real py_clob_client_v2 package), so if that library's L1/L2 auth failure raises an exception whose message embeds the invalid/rejected key (a common pattern in wallet-signing libraries), that message is not wrapped by any of live_client.py's own except blocks (LiveCredentials.load's CredentialsError messages only report the file path/mode, and LiveExecutor.execute's OrderSubmissionError deliberately omits str(exc) -- but nothing later in the chain prevents an exception raised outside those specific try blocks, e.g. during client construction in build_venue_client, from propagating uncaught straight to stderr). The net effect: the stated 'credentials never logged' boundary is enforced only for the subset of log lines that happen to be manually routed through safe_log_line, not for the process's actual stdout/stderr, which is precisely what launchd captures into the agent-readable directory.
evidence: ops/com.iamnotatrader.engine.plist: <key>StandardOutPath</key><string>.../runtime/logs/engine.log</string> and <key>StandardErrorPath</key><string>.../runtime/logs/engine.err.log</string> -- both inside runtime/, which docs/nanoclaw-setup.md's mount-allowlist example sets `"allowReadWrite": true` for and mounts into the agent container at /workspace/extra/trader-runtime.

engine/executor.py lines 14-18: 'Logging contract: every engine log line is produced by safe_log_line ... runtime/logs/ is agent-readable by design, so a leaked credential in a log line would defeat the credential-isolation decision.'

engine/market_feed.py line 29: `logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)`, with call sites at lines 175, 180, 185, 205, 212, 216, 291, 357 (`logger.warning(...)`) -- none of these go through safe_log_line, and grep across engine/, analytics/, ops/ finds zero `logging.basicConfig`/`addHandler`/`FileHandler` anywhere in the repo, so these fall back to Python's default stderr handler.

engine/main.py lines 241-266 (run_forever) and 347-379 (main/__main__): no try/except wraps build_loop()/startup()/run_forever() as a whole; only run_once (117-130) and _on_bucket_rollover (202-239) have local try/except that route errors through safe_log_line.

engine/live_client.py lines 378-480 (derive_l2, build_venue_client, _ClobV2Adapter) explicitly marked 'CAUTION -- vendor-API guesswork zone ... was NOT exercised against a live venue' and 'GUESS (verify in smoke test)'; docs/phase2-runbook.md Section 3 step 4 documents that wiring these into engine/main.py's startup path is still a pending manual code change, meaning this exact gap will be live the moment that documented change lands unless the logging contract is fixed first.
findings.suggested_fix: Wrap engine.main.main()'s entrypoint (or run_forever/startup/build_loop) in a top-level try/except that logs only a redacted summary through safe_log_line before exiting, rather than allowing Python's default excepthook to print raw exception text to stderr. Replace engine/market_feed.py's stdlib `logging.getLogger(__name__)` warnings with the same log_sink/safe_log_line contract used elsewhere (or install a logging.Handler whose emit() pipes the formatted message through safe_log_line and drops/escalates on a match, so the stdlib logging module can never bypass the contract). Before wiring LiveCredentials/derive_l2/build_venue_client into engine/main.py's startup path (per docs/phase2-runbook.md Section 3 step 4), add an explicit try/except around that call chain that raises a CredentialsError with a fixed, non-vendor-derived message (never `str(exc)` from the vendor library) so a vendor exception embedding the raw key can never reach any output stream.

residual_risks: engine/control.py's 'operator-approved' semantics for runtime/control/approved-lessons/*.md and the capital-affecting runtime/control/allocation-requests/*.json, control/vetoes/*, and veto-notice delivery_ack_ts edits are enforced only by the trader agent's skill prompt (skills/trader/SKILL.md: 'On explicit approval only ...', 'Only on explicit operator instruction ...'), not by any code-level check that a human actually authorized the write -- the engine's control-file processor accepts whatever file it finds in those directories. The design's actual authorization boundary is Telegram group membership plus agent prompt compliance (docs/nanoclaw-setup.md Section 7: 'group membership IS the authorization model'), which is explicitly acknowledged there but is worth re-flagging since a prompt-injected or malfunctioning agent session has direct filesystem authority to move allocations (bounded by risk.py's hard caps) and to pollute STRATEGY.md's Lessons Learned appendix with attacker-influenced text a future human or agent session may treat as trusted context.

engine/executor.py's SECRET_PATTERNS['hex_private_key'] intentionally requires a literal '0x' prefix (documented tradeoff to avoid false positives on numeric CLOB token ids), and LiveCredentials.load() does not itself enforce that POLYMARKET_PRIVATE_KEY is 0x-prefixed -- if an operator ever stores the key without that prefix, a 64-hex-char run without mixed case would not match any of the three SECRET_PATTERNS, silently narrowing the credential-detection backstop for that specific misconfiguration.

docs/nanoclaw-setup.md Section 1 documents that `nonMainReadOnly: false` (required for the runtime/ read-write mount to work) loosens the read-only clamp for ALL non-main NanoClaw groups on the host instance, not just the trader group -- the doc's own mitigation (every other allowlist root explicitly read-only) depends on that allowlist being maintained correctly over time as an operational control, not a code-enforced one.

engine/live_client.py's _ClobV2Adapter (order placement/status/cancel call shapes, response field names, auth factory kwargs) is explicitly unverified 'GUESS' code pending a live smoke test per docs/phase2-runbook.md Section 6; this is a correctness/safety risk to order-integrity assumptions (e.g. GTC-only enforcement, fee/price reconciliation) once real money is involved, independent of the logging issue above.
testing_gaps: No test exercises the process-wide logging guarantee end-to-end: existing tests validate safe_log_line as a pure function (tests/test_executor.py) but nothing asserts that engine.main's actual stdout/stderr stream (what launchd captures into runtime/logs/) never contains credential-shaped content when an exception occurs outside run_once's try/except (e.g. during startup/build_loop, or a future LiveCredentials/derive_l2 failure).

No test covers engine/market_feed.py's logger.warning() call sites for credential-shaped payloads or confirms they are intentionally excluded from the safe_log_line contract (they carry only public market-data content today, but nothing prevents a future call site there from logging something sensitive).

No integration/smoke test simulates a real or realistic vendor (py_clob_client_v2) exception from a malformed/rejected private key to confirm the resulting exception message never contains the raw key -- this is explicitly deferred to the (not-yet-executed) Phase 2 live smoke test per docs/phase2-runbook.md Section 6.
